tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, elevated bilirubin, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance and color, indicating possible renal and metabolic dysfunction. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic disturbances.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
